programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In the medieval Ethiopian city of Harar, Steve Backshall has a unique and potentially lethal encounter with a pack of spotted hyenas. Capable of tearing their prey apart with super-strong jaws and thick muscular bodies, here they are hand-fed, allowing Steve the encounter of a lifetime with this normally unapproachable predator. Heading out of the town and up into the mountains, Steve meets the rarest wolf on earth and discovers why a grass-munching monkey, the gelada baboon, has a set of teeth that would make a vampire jealous.

programme.title: The Mask
programme.desc: Mild-mannered bank clerk Stanley Ipkiss is a nice guy, but he's never had much success with women. Babes like sultry chanteuse Tina Carlyle have been way out of his league. Until now. For Stanley's discovered an ancient mask which will transform him into a green-faced, twinkle-toed charmer, who just thrives in the spotlight. Now the sky – and the lovely Tina – could be the limit...

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team is rocked when commissioner Selwyn Patterson is shot at the yacht club as he celebrates 50 years of police service.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley’s first appearance on TV, in the iconic series about the true cost of fashion, first broadcast in 2008. Six young fashion lovers swap shopping for the factories and backstreet workshops of India to learn how the clothes they wear are manufactured. They join the thousands of poverty stricken migrant labourers who head deep into the Indian countryside to work on the annual cotton harvest. The six hour, third-class train journey and life in the mill prove to be the group's most physically demanding challenges yet.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley’s first appearance on TV, in the iconic series about the true cost of fashion, first broadcast in 2008. Six young fashion lovers swap shopping for the factories and backstreet workshops of India to learn how the clothes they wear are manufactured. The group embark on the most shocking leg of their journey so far in Mumbai, as they are put to work in the backstreet factories of Dharivi, the largest slum in Asia. But when they stumble across child labour they are forced to delve a little deeper and find out the extent of the problem and what is being done to prevent it.

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: After prison, Coventry musician Pa Salieu returns with renewed purpose and takes the stage at Glastonbury. While he fights to rebuild his career, can music save him again?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May take an Aston DBS Volante, a Ferrari California and a Lamborghini Gallardo Spyder to Romania in search of an obscure stretch of tarmac that some hardcore drivers claim is nothing less than the greatest road in the world. Meanwhile, James makes a rare appearance on the track as he compares two absurdly powerful limos, the BMW 760i and Mercedes S63 AMG, and gets into a strange card game with the Stig. Star Trek, Troy and Hulk star Eric Bana is the Hollywood legend in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: It's week 9, and the candidates take on a classic task – TV selling. The teams select products which they hope will win over the nation. They then take to the airwaves, hoping to sell their socks off on a TV shopping channel – all while Lord Sugar watches on. There is a dramatic boardroom showdown, and one candidate risks it all to keep their place in the process.

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon and her special guests Lauren Layfield and Tre Lowe take a deep dive into the candidates’ performances and analyse the task, the boardroom and the firing. Angela also has an exclusive chat with the next person to leave the process.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The body of Giles, an eccentric monster hunter, is recovered from the sea. He bears injuries oddly suggestive of the legendary creature he has spent years chasing: the Lusca. The ever-rational Mervin Wilson dismisses the idea that there are supernatural elements to this case. But if this mythical beast from local folklore didn’t kill Giles, who, or what, did? Meanwhile, Mervin’s life is turned upside down by the presence of his mischievous niece, Eloise. But he has a difficult decision to make about whether to break some unwelcome news to the youngster about the father she clearly loves.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Ever wonder if doom-scrolling is draining your joy? You’re not alone. The science says cutting smartphone use to just two hours a day could seriously boost your wellbeing – but actually doing it is another story. So Zoe Ball is in Kettering to share with busy beautician Lucie some clever digital detox tricks that could improve family life, reduce stress and even boost her brain power. Less scrolling, more living.

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Prestwick’s Broadway Cinema hasn’t shown a film since its doors closed in 1976, so Mark Stuckey and Steve Rowley must battle rust, missing wires and obsolete carbon-arc lamps if they hope to bring its twin projectors back to life. Dom meets Shetlander Victoria, who needs her grandfather’s accordion fixed if she is to realise her busking ambitions, and then discovers the centuries-old art of enamelling watch dials, a craft that demands steady hands.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: It’s sun, sea and sand in this episode as Alistair Appleton helps a couple to trade in city life for a coastal home in Cornwall. With golden beaches and a nearby golf club high on their wish list, will they land a hole in one or get stuck in a sandy bunker? Along the way, Alistair encounters a mischievous ice cream-snatching seagull and visits a regenerative farm focused on nourishing land, mind and body.

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: It's the grand final! Hosts Bradley and Barney Walsh welcome back the four remaining, fantastic Contenders as they return to the arena to do battle one last time against the superhuman Gladiators, and each other. Over five punishing events, and then the iconic Eliminator, we will find out who has the speed, the strength and the heart to be crowned Gladiators Champion 2026. Who will have the will and the skill to conquer the dreaded Travelator one last time and claim the ultimate prize? In the final, Viper leaves the Gladiators gobsmacked, Bradley races Electro on the track, and much to Guy Mowbray’s surprise, Legend tries his hand at commentary.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete to score points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the Impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode, there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches as they attempt to knock their opponents out of the game and steal their cash. Only one player will be left standing at the end, but can they make it all the way to the Golden Run to double their money? Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clive swerves Tuscany’s famous posh villas to head to the medieval town of Barga, where over half the locals have Scottish connections. Staying in a mansion that’s now a Glaswegian-run B&B, Clive finds out why Italians love a fish supper, finds himself cooking dinner for 14, and joins an expedition to discover the forgotten quartz quarry that helped make Tuscany’s capital, Florence, so beautiful.

programme.title: Monkey Planet
programme.desc: The key to primate success is that, like us, most species live in close-knit family groups. How do you choose your friends, learn from those around you and know who to trust? We explore the complex world of primate social lifestyles. In Peru, emperor tamarin males are conned into childcare, while vervet monkeys in South Africa have a sophisticated alarm system to warn for predators. Elsewhere, George has a very close encounter with some hunting chimpanzees. But living in families is not always easy. In Gibraltar, barbary macaques steal babies to impress their boss. Hamadryas baboon males in Ethiopia rule with an iron fist, and in matriarchal ring-tailed lemur societies, the girls have to fight pitched battles with babies in tow.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine curios and artefacts offered up by the public. Among the turrets and terraces of Eastnor Castle in Herefordshire, they find some bizarre objects, including a wooden bicycle, a huge pocket knife with 96 implements, an embroidered egg, a World War I pack with a bullet lodged in it and the first All Blacks rugby shirt.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It! comes from Blackburn Cathedral in the heart of the town centre. The experts on duty today are Anita Manning and Philip Serrell. Anita values a gold charm bracelet, and Philip takes a shine to an unusual globe. Paul Martin learns about the remarkable life and work of classical singer Kathleen Ferrier.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Ahir Shah, Michaela Strachan, Dr Xand van Tulleken and Vogue Williams take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games suitcase, or will they be tempted with the House of Games bee hotel?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for Northern Ireland with blockbuster canapes, starters and fish dishes celebrating the British film industry. The dishes are judged by this week's veteran judges, 2025's banquet winners Sally Abe and Jean Delport, and include an homage to The School for Good and Evil that features a red knotted brioche handkerchief, and a tribute to director Lisa Barros D'Sa with barbecued monkfish served from a teapot. Who will impress, and who will be sent home?

programme.title: UEFA Women's Champions League
programme.desc: Chelsea v Arsenal in the second leg of their UEFA Women's Champions League quarter-final.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: This episode of the series is hosted by quick-witted Dara O Briain, who introduces wonderful Australian comic Felicity Ward and the sensational Mo Gilligan to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Adam Fleming.

programme.title: Captive Audience: A Real American Horror Story
programme.desc: A heroic escape. A televised family reunion. But the Stayner ordeal is far from over. When a hit TV movie rewrites the Stayner story, reality gets stranger than fiction.

programme.title: Belfast
programme.desc: 1969. Young Buddy’s happy home life is rocked by the eruption of the Troubles, on top of his concerns about ailing grandparents, a crush on a girl at school and doing maths. But street violence means the family face a stark future.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for Northern Ireland with blockbuster canapes, starters and fish dishes celebrating the British film industry. The dishes are judged by this week's veteran judges, 2025's banquet winners Sally Abe and Jean Delport, and include an homage to The School for Good and Evil that features a red knotted brioche handkerchief, and a tribute to director Lisa Barros D'Sa with barbecued monkfish served from a teapot. Who will impress, and who will be sent home?

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey is beyond excited to be attending a book signing by his favourite crime novelist, Kenneth Linder. But when Kenneth is found dead the next day, Humphrey is baffled to discover that the details of his demise eerily mirror the murder in his final novel. But is it a coincidence or murder by design? Meanwhile, Humphrey and Martha find themselves lost at sea when the Lily Bond is caught adrift. With their houseboat out of action, Humphrey and Martha soon find themselves back with Anne. Reluctant to live with her mother again, Martha is set on finding something more permanent, whilst Humphrey faces an impossible decision at the hub that will change his team for ever.

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.desc: Chaquie helps Rachel get ready for the big day. Claire challenges James over their co-parenting arrangement. It’s an important day for Anna, but tensions rise with Mammy, igniting an argument between the sisters and an emotional confrontation. Just when it feels as though everything is falling apart, can the sisters pull it back together?

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Edinburgh, 70-year-old Alister’s cycling accidents have resulted in a few knocks to his health and his confidence. Roman Kemp has a counterintuitive solution: easy exercise. It sounds backwards, but research shows that 'reverse' moves – like walking downhill instead of up, or lowering weights instead of lifting them – could deliver bigger gains in muscle strength, bone density and blood sugar control. Sometimes, the easy route really is the smart one.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse is in Sussex to help a couple from a Berkshire commuter town escape to the country. They have requested a unique home, and the quirkier, the better! The selection on offer includes some truly wow-factor properties, any one of which could be the mystery house. Denise also visits the Royal Botanic Gardens at Wakehurst to discover how climate change could shape the way we all garden in the future.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With blossom popping and new green shoots emerging across the garden, Monty is busy with spring and planning ahead for summer. He’s doing a seed-sowing masterclass, cutting back Cornus and dividing ferns to put in the new Woodland Garden. Frances Tophill is exploring the tiny but expansive world of miniature daffodils at a nursery with hundreds of cultivars. In Llanelli, Sue Kent meets a passionate gardener who has set up her own seed library, helping to preserve varieties of flowers and edibles which flourish in local Welsh soil and beyond. There’s a florist in West Sussex who specialises in sweet peas and a passionate tomato grower from Wiltshire who loves to grow old heirloom varieties that grew before the 1950s and have been gathered from around the world.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete to score points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the Impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode, there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches as they attempt to knock their opponents out of the game and steal their cash. Only one player will be left standing at the end, but can they make it all the way to the Golden Run to double their money? Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Italians are some of the happiest people in Europe, and Bologna, capital of the Emilia-Romagna region, is known as one of the happiest places in Italy. While the Pasta Sisters teach Clive how to make tortellini and reveal why even Italians think Bologna is the best place to eat, Clive dons his hard hat and scales some scaffolding to find out why this city is known as the medieval Manhattan.

programme.title: Monkey Planet
programme.desc: There's one thing that sets us primates apart from most other families on the planet, and that's a flexible mind. Our primate cousins are much smarter than you might imagine. Just like us they use tools, solve problems and even have emotions. Monkey Planet discovers how these animals are individuals with a sense of self and why brainpower is essential to primate survival. In Thailand, long-tailed macaques floss their teeth with human hair and use tools to open oysters on the beach. In Uganda, chimps pass on cultures and customs through generations. George McGavin goes to orangutan school in Sumatra and meets a bonobo in the States who can order his own picnic on a smart phone and toast marshmallows in a fire he makes himself.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine curios and artefacts offered up by the public. In this edition, the team return to Eastnor Castle in Herefordshire with expert Paul Atterbury, who surveys its Victorian interior and collections. And in a sequence of unseen clips from recent roadshows, exciting finds include a 17th-century wine bottle, suffragette memorabilia, a bust of General Gordon given to the owner's great-grandfather by Gordon's sister, and a much-loved Victorian dressing case.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It comes from the centre of historic Oxford. Our venue is the magnificent Sheldonian Theatre still used today for lectures and music recitals. Presenter Paul Martin is joined by antique experts Tracy Martin and Charlie Ross. Paul and Charlie get very enthusiastic about an arts and crafts ale jug and Tracy finds a very unusual gold scarab. Paul visits the drawing and print room at the Ashmolean Museum.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Ahir Shah, Michaela Strachan, Dr Xand van Tulleken and Vogue Williams take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games suitcase, or will they be tempted with the House of Games bee hotel?

programme.title: Rick Stein’s Food Stories
programme.desc: Amid Belfast’s buzzy food scene, Rick tucks into Filipino-style Sunday lunch. On Northern Ireland’s Atlantic coast, he meets an award-winning chef with a ‘no waste’ approach who turns fish offal and underused species such as dogfish into stunning dishes. Rick makes his first ever Filipino dish, and after witnessing the dry-ageing of some special Irish beef, he cooks beef and Guinness pie.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three talented chefs from Northern Ireland are halfway through their heat, and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating the British film industry and films with links to Northern Ireland. The dishes are judged by veteran judges Jean Delport and Sally Abe. They include a roast chicken tribute to Kenneth Branagh's adaptation of Murder on the Orient Express, as well as two desserts inspired by the film Good Vibrations. But who will be leaving the competition?

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Callum needs to haggle hard to get a good price for his calves from a mainland buyer, while Zara’s morning cattle check brings devastating news. Andrew feels under pressure to make the farm pay more so that his dad can take a step back, but right now it's all hands to the pump while pregnant Gayle has been signed off work. And Noah wants a future in farming, but he needs to balance lambing with exam revision.

programme.title: Kegworth: Flight to Disaster
programme.desc: The air disaster that changed flight safety. Survivors and eyewitnesses tell their story of the terrifying moment Flight BD092 crashed into the side of the M1 motorway.

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Lord Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her special guests take a deep dive into the candidates' performances and analyse the task, the boardrooms and the firings. Plus, an exclusive chat with the next person to leave the process. This week's guests are Claudine Collins and Owain Wyn Evans.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Joe Pike.

programme.title: Clash of the Superpowers: America vs China
programme.desc: Taiwan becomes a dangerous flashpoint as the battle for supremacy ramps up. Trump’s first term ends with relations with China at their lowest point for years as both countries deal with the devastating consequences of the Covid pandemic – or, as Trump called it, ‘the China plague.’ Xi’s government have high hopes for Trump’s successor, Joe Biden, but these are dashed after a frosty meeting in Alaska. Biden sparks further anger in Beijing when he signs a nuclear submarine deal with Australia and the UK, and takes on China over tech – before speaker Nancy Pelosi’s visit to Taiwan brings the two superpowers closer than ever to military confrontation. Tensions remain high as Donald Trump takes over for his second term, throwing world politics into disarray. Top interviewees include US national security adviser Jake Sullivan, speaker of the House of Representatives Nancy Pelosi, UK prime minister Boris Johnson, Australian prime minister Scott Morrison and Taiwan’s foreign minister David Lee, as well as top Chinese academics who give the inside track from Beijing.

programme.title: Inside Britain's National Parks
programme.desc: Once a royal hunting ground for William the Conqueror, the New Forest is now a unique mosaic of open heathlands, ancient woodlands, coastline and picturesque villages. The national park is home to the New Forest’s famous ponies, deer, rare bird species and reptiles. The traditional grazing practices of generations of commoners sustain this unique landscape, with their ponies and pigs roaming freely throughout the park.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three talented chefs from Northern Ireland are halfway through their heat, and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating the British film industry and films with links to Northern Ireland. The dishes are judged by veteran judges Jean Delport and Sally Abe. They include a roast chicken tribute to Kenneth Branagh's adaptation of Murder on the Orient Express, as well as two desserts inspired by the film Good Vibrations. But who will be leaving the competition?

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary prepares for her official debut in London society, buoyed by her friendship with Ann and the attentions of Mr Ryder. But a run-in with old rival Caroline Bingley threatens Mary’s growing confidence. Mary faces up to Caroline at a dinner party, but just as things seem to be going in the right direction for Mary, an urgent message from home throws everything into uncertainty.

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary rushes to Pemberley, fearing her mother is gravely ill, only to be thrown back into the demands of family life. Mary begins to lose hope of ever returning to London – until Mr Ryder unexpectedly turns up. His clear admiration for Mary helps her family recognise how much she has changed. As excitement mounts over Mr Ryder’s visit, Mary finds herself on the brink of a momentous decision.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Clive Myrie is in Kent with Tracey, a soon-to-be grandmother who is worried about her high blood pressure. His suggestion? Borrow a pet – yes, really. With new apps making it simple to match people with animals, Tracey could enjoy all the benefits without the long-term commitment. And the science is clear: spending time with animals boosts happiness, improves wellbeing and lowers the risk of dying from cardiovascular disease. A furry friend might be just what her heart needs.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel and Nitro are duelling with each other over ideas at a studio flat in London. We delve into the archives all the way back to 2009, when Martin was charmed by a Grade II listed house in Devon. Martin is also in Somerset, where another character property is ripe for reinvention.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dean restores a pair of shoes with a surprising past, Mark revives a retro recording device, and Sonnaz and Angelina pair up to salvage a battered survivor of heady days in London.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Julie Hesmondhalgh and her niece Rosa present an appeal on behalf of Ovarian Cancer Action, a charity funding vital research to improve early diagnosis, develop better treatments and ultimately save more women’s lives.

programme.title: King of Kings
programme.desc: This biblical epic concentrates on three main elements of the New Testament story - the life of Christ, the tensions between Herod's court and the Roman administration culminating in the execution of John the Baptist and, finally the story of Barabbas.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete to score points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the Impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode, there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches as they attempt to knock their opponents out of the game and steal their cash. Only one player will be left standing at the end, but can they make it all the way to the Golden Run to double their money? Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Coast ventures to the farthest-flung reaches of the British Isles to explore Life Beyond the Edge. Mark Horton is in Liverpool to meet an old friend. Brunel's famous ship, the Great Eastern, now lies beneath the mud, but Mark is resurrecting the story of her greatest triumph - how she conquered the Atlantic by connecting Britain's edge to America with 2,000 miles of telegraph wire. And Andy Torbet is on the island of Lewis attempting to follow in the footsteps of the sea shepherds, a style of farming that is slowly dying out.

programme.title: Funeral in Berlin
programme.desc: Harry Palmer is sent to Berlin with instructions to assist Colonel Stok, the Russian head of security for the Berlin Wall, who is eager to defect to the West. However, Palmer discovers he cannot trust anyone in the city where the double cross is a unit of currency.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine curios and artefacts offered up by the public in Eston, Cleveland. Exciting finds include a sculpted elephant by Eduardo Paolozzi used to promote floor covering; a Victorian painting bought for five shillings while sheltering from the rain; the first commercially produced toy robot made in the 1930s; a collection of craft jewellery found in an old envelope at the end of a jumble sale; and a fibre-glass chair which Paul Atterbury says is 'an antique for the future'.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It! is at Blackburn Cathedral in the centre of Blackburn in Lancashire. The cathedral was completed in 1926, formally the Church of St Mary. Experts Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Charles values a wooden Fijian club, while two vintage enamel signs catch Philip’s eye. Paul Martin takes a look at the impressive Talbot collection – local press photographs taken of events, celebrities and the royal family over a 50-year period.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Ahir Shah, Michaela Strachan, Dr Xand van Tulleken and Vogue Williams take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games suitcase, or will they be tempted with the House of Games bee hotel?

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Brian Cunningham are back at Beechgrove to kick off the season with a range of great jobs that can be done right now.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Northern Ireland must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They need to impress a panel of exacting judges: Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge, former Great British Menu Champion of Champions Lorna McNee, and comedian and all-round food enthusiast Phil Wang. The guest judge is award-winning Belfast director Lisa Barros D’Sa, whose films Ordinary Love and Good Vibrations have already been celebrated in dishes earlier in the week. How will she judge dishes celebrating the British film industry? Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent Northern Ireland at the national finals.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With a long Easter weekend to look forward to, Monty is on hand with wheelbarrows of practical gardening advice and inspiration. For people who fancy growing their own potatoes, it's time to get them in the ground. He’ll also be sowing seeds for a colourful cut flower display and starting a new project to show how easy and inexpensive it is to create topiary from yew cuttings. Ashley Edwards heads to a magical spring garden in Horsham to share his love of the season of renewal. In Carmarthenshire, there’s a grower who gave up her job in London to follow her dream of becoming a flower farmer. Plus a gardener in Ely who is simply potty about pots.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Change sweeps across Xudum, and every big cat character must adapt to survive. The core of the Xudum lion pride has moved south, but a group of its young, opportunistic females has remained in the north. The crew is excited to find one of these lionesses has cubs, but as a first-time mum, she has a lot to learn. Cheetah mum Pobe experiences tragedy when a leopard attacks, but hope returns when her three-year-old daughter Neelo is found and is thriving. Lediba the leopard shows us there is more to her than meets the eye, but she’s under increasing pressure to catch large prey.

programme.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
programme.desc: Based on the true story of stockbroker Jordan Belfort, whose rise and fall involved drug-fuelled decadence, corruption and the making of a fortune from shady deals.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Two years ago, the government banned XL bully dogs, promising it would save lives. Panorama investigates whether that commitment has been kept. Attacks are still happening, the police say the law is difficult to enforce, and some animal charities say they are overwhelmed with abandoned dogs. Reporter Sam Poling hears from families who have lost loved ones after XL attacks and from owners struggling to stay on the right side of the law.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Bitzer treats the farmer to a restaurant style dining experience, offering a delicious pie as the main course. But when the pie goes missing, Bitzer makes the mistake of accepting Shaun and the flock's offer of help in the kitchen.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The Princess is back and erases all weekdays so she can play at the weekend forever. Danger Mouse must ruin everyone's lives by fixing the problem!

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Mr Friendlyface is particularly tetchy this morning. He is packing for a skiing trip, but a dragon has pinched his golden fleece, and he urgently needs the heroes to quest it back – how else will he stay warm and cosy on the slopes? However, when the heroes accidentally agree to let his niece Trixie tag along with them, they fear that her clumsiness and lack of hero skills will ruin everything.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: A dog show is coming up, and cool prizes are given to the winning dog and its trainer. Cheese, Girl and Mouse all have great ideas for how to train Dog for the grand finale. And if Dog picks one of their finales, that sibling gets to be the trainer who shares in the prize! Boy also wants to help but gets shunned for having a less impressive idea on how to win. The contest arrives, but Dog is torn when it comes to his finale. Whose idea does he choose? Can Boy jump in with his 'unimpressive' idea and help save the day?

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Transformed into a fairy, a lemming organises a magic show and gets rid of Grizzy.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: A prehistoric sponge is unfrozen and causes chaos.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigates strange events. When a new room opens up in headquarters, all the agents want a piece.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: When Olive surprises herself and everyone around her by actually becoming a good cook, Dr Pfooflepfeffer tries to cash in by turning the school canteen into an exclusive and super-expensive dining experience.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: Lance is a ping pong whizz, he always aces the annual Pottsfield Ping Pong competition. This year Danny’s determined to teach him a thing or two and has stayed up all night watching online tutorials. Now he can’t keep his eyes open long enough to play! Lance smells victory, Mick smells trouble. Mick is determined to find Danny somewhere to get a nap. However, with Joy on the prowl and Lance making things difficult it looks like Danny may never lift that trophy!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Patrick wants to be a horse!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck gets a job in a cinema projection room, Frog manages to accidentally break the projector. Can Frog save the day and entertain the audience himself?

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog are working on a train, but when Frog lets it slip that there is a celebrity on board, the rest of the passengers cause chaos as they try to get a picture.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a compass that indicates the direction of one's own desires.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a sceptre that covers anything it touches in gold.

programme.title: What's New Scooby-Doo?
programme.desc: The gang are in Mexico trying to stop a giant sand worm from wreaking havoc at a race.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob doesn't need a licence to operate a self-driving car!

programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick exchange tiny living versions of themselves.

programme.desc: Max’s disdain for his new automatic cat feeder leads to a machine revolution.

programme.title: Girl Troop vs Aliens
programme.desc: When Orbit’s plan moves too slowly, Tilda takes charge and teams up with an unlikely ally. But shapeshifting aliens and hidden traps make her mission a lot more complicated.

programme.title: High Hoops
programme.desc: When the Vipers arrive at the wrong venue for a match, they are forced to take part in an athletics meet. Can Aoife appease Holt and win the school a trophy?

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Chris and Dr Xand explore what happens when you vomit. Dr Raph disappears into Dr Dan’s body to see what his heart does in Just One Second, and we meet a brand new Oucher.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Mr Friendlyface is acting very strangely indeed. He refuses to stamp any of Admin Bunny’s documents, keeps getting distracted by butterflies, and seems oddly happy! The heroes are extremely suspicious and decide that someone must have swapped him with a doppelganger. Now they must quest to find out where the real Friendlyface is and get him back before the forest descends into total chaos.

programme.title: Ninja Express
programme.desc: When their dear friend and very first customer, Bonnie, orders a tent, the Ninjas travel down memory lane as they recount their first meeting and first ever delivery!

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Piper and Amy's secret plan is put into action, as the dancers rally to unite the studios under one banner. Amy's future at the studio is threatened when her mother makes a difficult demand. Emily and Michelle are forced to face their shortcomings.

programme.title: High Hoops
programme.desc: When Aoife needs a cheerleading teacher to impress her auntie Fidelma, she finds help from an unlikely source - but can she keep up the act?

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall is in Brazil searching for the world’s most elusive cat: the jaguar. He sees them swimming, meets mothers and cubs, and even witnesses one jaguar hunting a caiman.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: The Farmer receives a mystery gift from his antipodean cousin. Unimpressed with the present, he attempts to throw it away, only for it to keep reappearing. He becomes convinced it has magical powers.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Eager to prove her babysitting ability for a permit, Gran’s on the hunt for a baby stand-in. Walter is suspiciously all too willing to offer up Bertie. Can the gang help Gran wrangle the reluctant baby-sitt-ee?

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The Gnome Crystal Mine has ground to a halt, leaving the forest with no crystals, no power supply and melting ice lollies! The heroes are tasked with a quest to find out what has happened and to fix it. It turns out that a slimy beast named Smarg has made the mine her home. But with Hoofius’s fear of gunk getting the better of him, evicting the slippery Smarg is not going to be easy.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Girl is failing science class, so Mouse offers to help her learn. However, Girl decides it will be easier to borrow one of his inventions and cheat instead. Everything is going to plan until the borrowed invention goes wrong and causes a scientific calamity!

programme.desc: Grizzy snares a swordfish, but the Lemmings lure it out to sea. The cabin starts to slide.

programme.desc: Duck and Frog have one simple job: press buttons in a lift - and they can’t even do that right. Can they get the mayor to the ground floor in time for a grand opening?

programme.desc: When looking after their neighbours' baby, Duck soon learns that he is a terrible babysitter - though it seems kids love Frog! Duck needs Frog to help out for once but soon loses the baby.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Herbert is really proud of the plants he has grown ready for a visit from The Complete Gnome Gardener magazine. Now, the whole forest is at risk of being overwhelmed by the smell, and the heroes must quest to get rid of it.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: After her siblings accidentally insult her dreams of being a pop star, Cheese is bonked on the head and starts to believe she's a huge celebrity! Cheese turns into a diva, making outrageous demands from her siblings, and soon enough they're sick of it. But when they learn she was duping them all along, they set out to teach her a lesson...

programme.desc: Professor von Screwtop gets mistaken for the new kid in school and joins Class 3C for the day. Rubi is so embarrassed at her dad’s antics that she resorts to pranking to get him into trouble and out of the way.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: Danny and Mick’s dad Clive has turned up with his latest in a long line of ‘sure-fire’ money making schemes: washing customers’ cars in the car park while they use the leisure centre. The boys are happy to help and are soon scrubbing away, but when Joy finds out and demands a free wash, the boys clean her beloved old banger so hard it falls to bits. Don’t worry lads, Clive knows how to fix this… or at least replace one problem with a much bigger problem. Yeah, thanks dad!

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: How does blood carry oxygen? Dr Tosin sends Dr Xand and Dr Chris through blood vessel tunnels to find out. And Dr Raph meets robots being trialled for magnetic vine surgery.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Animated comedy about Bob Belcher, who runs Bob's Burgers restaurant, and his family. When Linda takes to caring for an injured raccoon behind the restaurant's dumpster during a block party, she regrets calling Animal Control after Teddy tells her that they might kill the poor animal - meanwhile, a live concert is set up in front of the restaurant but the family can't see the stage, a factor which complicates Louise's plan to let friends watch it from her bedroom window in exchange for sweets.

programme.title: Piglets
programme.desc: Sitcom set in a fictional police training college. The excited recruits get to wear their uniforms for the first time, Dev reveals a deeply personal issue that Steph offers to help with, and Paul gets a second shot at his mission.

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Sitcom about three desperate young men who reside in ancient Rome. Marcus's parents are visiting for the weekend and he is keen to keep them and Delphine as far apart as possible - as Stylax pursues a romance with Ursula, one of the Vestal Virgins who guard the city's sacred flame, Grumio finds some mouldy cheese under his bed and spots a business opportunity.

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection. [S]

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Animated series based on the DC Comics superhero team. As commercials are the only part of the show that he likes, Control Freak creates extra advertisements to torment the Titans.

programme.title: Looney Tunes Cartoons
programme.desc: Animated series inspired by the classic Looney Tunes shorts. Porky Pig and Daffy Duck go diving for pearls, Elmer Fudd has a hunting mishap, and Wile E. Coyote gets a new suit.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated
programme.desc: Animated adventures with Scooby and the gang. A brand-new theatre production is under threat by a mummified corpse from Mystery Cove's ancient past, so Scooby and the gang are drafted in by director Vincent Van Ghoul to help investigate the mysterious goings-on and ensure that the show goes ahead as planned.

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: Animated adventure series following Shaggy, Scooby, Velma, Daphne and Fred as they travel around on what could be their final summer break together. In order to solve the mystery of a ghostly performer haunting her former venue, the gang has to pretend to be a rock band themselves - can they solve the case before Velma has to actually get on stage and sing?

programme.title: Dress To Impress
programme.desc: Ash, Isabel and Helen all hope to impress cleaner and musician Jodie from Milton Keynes with their style choices.

programme.title: Billie & Greg: The Family Diaries
programme.desc: Reality series. With Chatty in a great place and the summer holidays in full swing, Billie and Greg throw a fun-filled barbecue with friends and family to take their mind off things - elsewhere, Billie and Sue visit Xanthe and baby Hugo where Xanthe has an exciting proposal for Billie, Greg and friend Sam have their hands full in Essex looking after Margot and Arthur when Margot's potty training doesn't quite go to plan, Sam comes over with the kids for a much-needed catch-up with Billie, and Chatty pops in en route to rehab for an emotional reunion with the whole family.

programme.title: The Only Way is Essex
programme.desc: There is cause for celebration in Essex as Dani finally passes her driving test on her seventh attempt - to celebrate Dani's success, Saff throws her a congratulatory party and includes Junaid on the invite list, but Dani is not impressed that Junaid is there. Picking up on the awkward atmosphere, Junaid decides to leave but not before congratulating his old friend - Saff witnesses this conversation and decides to leave the party with Junaid. Elsewhere at Dani's party, tensions also simmer when Liv and Freddie come face to face for the first time since their heated conversation - Liv approaches Freddie and admits that things got out of control as the two try to bury the hatchet. Meanwhile, Saff and Ella bump into Wills at his flower stall and the girls have a bit of flirty banter with him - Ella tells Saff that Wills is a bit of her and Saff admits that she still finds him sexy. The girls joke that they can fight for him, but they also know that Elma has caught his eye too.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Atlanta
programme.desc: A Whine of a Time: US reality series. The ladies head to the Toronto Carnival in Canada and Porsha has a few things to get off her chest before the festivities begin. [S] S12 Ep9

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills
programme.desc: Follow the lavish lives of six savvy champagne-swilling Beverly Hills women who were born for shopping and partying! The ladies of Beverly Hills reunite to work through hurt feelings and shocking allegations on the heels of a tumultuous year - Dorit speaks out about her separation and feeling unsupported by Kyle as a surprise statement leaves her at a loss for words, Bozoma responds to those questioning her relationship with Keely and accusations that she was being manipulated by Dorit, and Garcelle has to answer for hurtful insinuations she made throughout the series.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revamp of the iconic game show where three pairs of shoppers go wild in the aisles as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a 3,000-pound prize - along with the classic Supermarket Sweep quizzes, trolley dashes and pastel jumpers, the revamped show features exciting new games, challenges and some famous faces.

programme.title: Wheel of Fortune
programme.desc: Graham Norton invites AJ Odudu, Gok Wan and Charlotte Church to try their luck at spinning the Wheel and solving puzzles to win big for charity.

programme.title: The Only Way is Essex
programme.desc: Love is in the air in Essex as it's Junaid and Joe's one-year anniversary - they celebrate with a night away in a lodge and reflect on their relationship and how far they've come. When they return home, there is a package waiting for them from Harry - as the pair work out if it is a goodwill gesture or if it will sour their celebrations, Saff, Ella, Elma and Wills play padel and the new lothario finds that he has got his work cut out with these three strong single women. Elsewhere, Diags opens up to Dan about his future with Jodie - things have been looking good and they have been happier than ever, but not everything may be as rosy as it seems. Meanwhile, Amy and Jordan decide to stage an intervention between Harry and Saff as the pair have been on bad terms since the Portugal trip - Harry and Saff have always managed to resolve their issues, but they may have gone too far this time to find a way back.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Cheshire
programme.desc: Reality series. Dawn Ward is back again, and this time she joins Rachel to jump headfirst out of a plane - unfortunately, it's a bumpy ride for Rachel in more ways than one while an orchestrated lunch fails to resolve growing tensions between Paige and herself. When then at Sheena's perfume launch, Lystra finally airs a long-standing grudge that shocks Rachel - and leaves Lystra furious.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Atlanta
programme.desc: Living on the Edge: US reality series. NeNe receives a frosty reception when she arrives in Toronto. A group trust exercise forces the ladies to live on the edge. [S] S12 Ep10

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills
programme.desc: Follow the lavish lives of six savvy champagne-swilling Beverly Hills women who were born for shopping and partying! The 14th series reunion continues as Kyle and Dorit face off over Kyle's text to PK and put more strain on their already fragile friendship, Boz stands firm on her claim that one of the women is not telling the truth, the fabulous Jennifer Tilly joins the action - but it isn't all diamonds and caviar when she is called out for her shady comments - and Sutton is put in the hot seat by the arrival of Kathy Hilton and for short-changing the size of Dorit's wallet.

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show with Les Dennis, Rebecca Sarker, and Jordan Stephens all trying to 'say what they see' as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a 50,000-pound jackpot for their chosen charity.

programme.title: Wheel of Fortune
programme.desc: Graham Norton invites celebrities Tom Allen, Michelle Visage and Eddie Kadi to spin the Wheel to get the chance to play for 50,000 pounds for charity.

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Have you ever wondered how clever you are? programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: The American version of Gordon Ramsay's culinary boot camp. There's a clash of culinary identities this time as the chefs are tasked with blending two distinct US state delicacies into one harmonious dish - later, both teams must come together for a high-pressure dinner service packed with VIP guests that includes a rowdy bachelor party and legendary University of Connecticut (UConn) basketball stars. With too many cooks in the kitchen, egos flare and tempers boil over.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Anarchic animated sitcom about a dysfunctional family in a wacky Rhode Island town. The two continue to date in secret - that is, until Nugget sneaks into Meg's room and accidentally reveals himself to Peter.

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Bob's Burgers

programme.desc: Love is in the air in Essex as it's Junaid and Joe's one-year anniversary - they celebrate with a night away in a lodge and reflect on their relationship and how far they've come. When they return home, there is a package waiting for them from Harry - as the pair work out if it is a goodwill gesture or if it will sour their celebrations, Saff, Ella, Elma and Wills play padel and the new lothario finds that he has got his work cut out with these three strong single women. Elsewhere, Diags opens up to Dan about his future with Jodie - things have been looking good and they have been happier than ever, but not everything may be as rosy as it seems. Meanwhile, Amy and Jordan decide to stage an intervention between Harry and Saff as the pair have been on bad terms since the Portugal trip - Harry and Saff have always managed to resolve their issues, but they may have gone too far this time to find a way back.

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: Crime drama. East Sussex CID emerges from the ashes of a shocking betrayal and prepares for a major criminal trial - after Grace's team intercepted millions of pounds of cocaine being smuggled into Newhaven port in a case where courier Daniel Starr worked for an elusive figure known as The Merchant, who had evaded identification for years, Starr gave up The Merchant's identity and Grace is determined to see him go down for his crimes as the ruthless drug lord faces trial. Meanwhile, Brighton resident Meg Magellan's daughter Laura leaves for a gap year and Meg receives a jury summons which brings with it a terrifying dilemma - as the trial unravels, Grace becomes convinced that The Merchant is trying to destabilise the case against him and must work doggedly to find whoever is doing his dirty work. As a double murder at a local park throws his team into a puzzling case, Grace is pushed to the brink to see justice done before an unwelcome conversation burdens him with a terrible secret.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A man is found in the middle of a crop circle, naked, with two puncture wounds on his back and the back of his head shaved. A Ufologist claims extra terrestrial involvement but DCI Tom Barnaby looks for a more plausible explanation. When a series of bodies are found in crop circles in the cornfields of Sir Harry Chatwyn, squire of the village of Midsomer Parva, it looks like a case of extra terrestrial kidnapping. Two bodies, bearing the classic hallmarks of alien abduction burnt fingers, a puncture wound to the lower back and a chunk cut out of the hair - seem to provide evidence that UFOs do exist. But when Inspector Barnaby and Sergeant Troy dig deeper, it seems there could be a far more simple explanation for these deaths cold blooded murder motivated by greed, love and jealousy.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Lewis and Hathaway investigate the death of Professor Miranda Thornton after she apparently killed herself when her dating video was leaked onto a vicious media blog - Lewis does not believe that Thornton's death was suicide and has qualms about Kit Renton, the blog's founder, and Sophie Leland, the owner of the dating website. When one of Renton's employees is found murdered, the police realise she must have been killed because she knew something about Thornton's death - as Lewis finds that Thornton, Renton and Leland were all at Oxford together 20 years before, he realises that this is actually an age-old revenge story...

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal who torment the long-suffering inspector of the Luxton Bus Company. Arthur is attracted to Beryl, a clippie on his regular bus - however, Beryl has only two interests in life, men with a fondness for athletics and those with a good head of hair.

programme.title: Never the Twain
programme.desc: Comedy series about two rival antique dealers. Oliver accepts Simon's challenge to take up judo - and soon regrets his decision.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Classic sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Everyone wants to go when Mum wins a holiday for two to the Costa Brava.

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Classic '70s sitcom with Sid James. Mike urges his parents to spend a weekend living on bread and water and giving the money saved on food to charity, but Sid finds it quite difficult.

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Classic '70s sitcom with Sid James. Jean hatches a plan to catch Sid out when she gets worried about the many flirty women that he meets in his line of work.

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Classic '70s sitcom with Sid James. An old flame from Jean's youth comes back into her life and asks her out to dinner - Sid is surprisingly fine with this... or is he?

programme.title: George & Mildred
programme.desc: Domestic sitcom spin-off from the hit '70s show Man About the House. George is ordered to watch his table manners when Mildred's sister comes round for dinner.

programme.title: Steptoe and Son Ride Again
programme.desc: The second feature-length spin-off from the classic sitcom. Things are bad at the Steptoes' Shepherd's Bush rag-and-bone business - so bad, in fact, that Harold, who is always on the lookout for ways to improve his lot, invests his father's life savings in a greyhound who is almost blind and can't see the hare. When this plot fails, Harold is convinced that Albert must die so he can collect the old man's life insurance, so he asks him to play along with his latest bright idea...

programme.title: Carry On... Don't Lose Your Head
programme.desc: British comedy in which the Carry On team have an irreverent poke at the French Revolution. In 1789 Paris, two English noblemen called Sir Rodney Ffing and Lord Darcy take it upon themselves to rescue their French counterparts from the clutches of the revolting masses, Citizen Robespierre, and the notorious Madame Guillotine. The Black Fingernail and his sidekick prove elusive, but Camembert - the chief of the secret police - and his comrade Bidet are determined to track them down...

programme.title: Carry On Girls
programme.desc: Bawdy British comedy. In one of the later Carry On films, local businessman and councillor Sidney Fiddler convinces the town council of a rundown seaside resort to stage a beauty contest by persuading them that it will be a marvellous publicity stunt - however, the idea meets with fervent opposition from the local Women's Liberation group, who are led by the fearsome Councillor Prodworthy, as they attempt to sabotage the contest.

programme.title: Carry On Dick
programme.desc: Bawdy British comedy. It is 1750 and King George has set up the Bow Street Runners to combat the increase in crime - many highwaymen are captured, but not Dick Turpin, the most notorious of all. While disguised as a rector, his maid, and an organ player respectively, Dick and his accomplices are terrorising the countryside around Upper Dencher. Sir Roger Daley is determined to catch them at all costs with the help of Captain Fancey and Sergeant Jock Strapp, but will they escape Roger's grasp?

programme.title: Carry On Abroad
programme.desc: Classic British comedy. A package holiday abroad turns into a hilarious adventure for some unlucky holidaymakers - staying at the shoddy Palace Hotel on the island of Elsbels, they are disrupted by bad plumbing, a restaurant filled with mosquitoes, and a dawn chorus of cement mixers. All of this is on top of several intermarital and extramarital complications and misunderstandings during the stay - will all be well, or will all be hell? Innuendos and gags abound in this very British romp.

programme.title: Carry On Matron
programme.desc: Classic British comedy with the Carry On team about a gang's plan to raid a maternity hospital to steal a gigantic stock of birth control pills. One of the gang gets the lie of the land by disguising himself as a nurse, but the hospital's hypochondriac head doctor - who begins to make amorous advances towards him - and a formidable matron prove constant obstacles to the heist.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Comedy-drama series about a GP who relocates to the sleepy Cornish hamlet of Portwenn. Martin is called to Louisa's new home after she has a dizzy spell while shifting furniture - Martin urges her to slow down but is then shocked to learn that Louisa has applied to be her school's headteacher again. Martin is asked to join the interview panel for the headteacher position as a school governor, but his invitation is withdrawn when he makes it clear that he thinks that Louisa is now unsuited for the role - meanwhile, the Doc treats an elegant elderly couple who want a disabled persons' parking permit so they can park near the shops, Al needs some money fast and offers to sort out all Louisa's household and plumbing problems if she can pay him before the weekend, the school secretary comes to Martin complaining of hay fever, and Edith brings Martin an ingenious gift to help him get the surgeon's job in London - however, the well-intended present ends up having unexpected consequences...

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Comedy-drama series about a GP who relocates to the sleepy Cornish hamlet of Portwenn. Martin visits London to meet with Robert Dashwood, the leader of the selection process for the prestigious London surgeon's job that he has applied for - while on the train back to Cornwall, Martin is annoyed to be seated next to local sheep farmer Jim Selkirk. When Jim slumps onto Martin's shoulder after he falls asleep, Martin discovers that he has died of heart failure - after returning to Portwenn, Martin is visited by Jim's widow as she looks for emotional comfort. When the widow then starts to see visions of her dead husband and collapses in a sheep pen, Martin is able to uncover the medical reason behind her hallucinations - meanwhile, Joan's punishment of a naughty schoolboy on an educational trip leads to a threatening letter from a solicitor when the boy's family accuse her of causing an illness in their son. Can the Doc identify the real source of the sickness and clear Joan's name?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. The brutal murder of a village postman leads Barnaby to a hotbed of adultery within remote Goodman's Land - as more murders rock the tiny hamlet, can community WPC Jay Nash help untangle the web of sexual liaisons to find the killer?

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Miss Marple is shocked when her old friend Father Gorman is brutally murdered after visiting a dying parishioner. She is further surprised when she receives a letter from Gorman, sent moments before his death, which contains a list of names. When Detective Inspector Lejeune dismisses the list, Miss Marple is determined to seek justice for her friend herself. She finds another copy of the list at the parishioner's home, and a clue which leads her to The Pale Horse, an inn run by three modern-day witches who claim to be able to kill using black magic. While in the area, she attends a ritual 'burning' in memory of famous witch Goody Carne and meets locals including folklore historian Mark Easterbrook. Then a guest at The Pale Horse is found dead, and Miss Marple fears there may be worse to come. She must go to dangerous lengths to uncover the truth behind the mystery. Is magic really someone's weapon of choice or is there something even more sinister at work?

programme.title: TATA IPL 2025 HLs RR v GT

programme.title: Aag Se Khelenge
programme.desc: Shekhar teams up with Johnny to fight Zaka's empire. But when he discovers Ravi Saxena died months ago, everything he believed about his ally - and their mission - starts to unravel.

programme.title: Don (1978)
programme.desc: Don, a wanted criminal, succumbs to his injuries in a police chase. Things take a turn when Vijay, a lookalike of Don, replaces him and tries to trace the details of his illegal activities.

programme.title: Bomb Blast
programme.desc: Jai Kishen is an aspiring police officer who marries his ladylove against his parents' wishes. MLA Sadakhare hires some men to carry out a bomb blast.

programme.title: Mr. Bond

programme.title: Shaadi Mein Zaroor Aana

programme.title: Sanam Teri Kasam
programme.desc: Sanam Teri Kasam

programme.title: Seetimaarr
programme.desc: Starring: Gopichand,Tamannaah. A bank employee and kabaddi coach aims to uplift his father's school and lead village players to national victory, but challenges abound.

programme.title: Uri: The Surgical Strike
programme.desc: Starring: Vicky Kaushal,Paresh Rawal. After militants attack a base in Uri, Major Vihaan Singh Shergill executes a covert strike across the border to eliminate the perpetrators with tactical precision.

programme.title: Bhairathi Ranagal
programme.desc: Starring: Shiva Rajkumar,Rahul Bose. Bhairathi Ranagal explores Ranagal's journey-his origin, rise to power, and transformation into the most feared gangster of Ronapura, revealing who he was before the notoriety.

programme.title: Encounter Shankar
programme.desc: Starring: Mahesh Babu,Tamannaah Bhatia. Shankar is an orphan child who is cared for by police officer Raja Rao. When the police officer's son Bharath kills a child, he takes the blame for it. Years later, he becomes an encounter specialist.

programme.title: Bachchhan Paandey
programme.desc: Starring: Akshay Kumar,Kriti Sanon. To succeed, Myra focuses her film on notorious gangster Bachchhan Paandey. The plot thickens when Paandey demands to act in the film, creating chaos and raising stakes for everyone involved.

programme.title: Ghajini
programme.desc: Starring: Aamir Khan,Asin Thottumkal. After a brutal attack, Sanjay's memory resets every fifteen minutes. Armed with tattooed notes, he seeks his fiancee's killer, navigating a labyrinth of clues before time resets his investigation.
